column3: ALT. — all caps, full stop retained. Full stop is active punctuation, not decoration. Signals completion and deliberateness.
column4: Abbreviated structure requires all-caps to hold presence at small scale. Full stop makes the name a statement, not a label.
column5: Full stop must be retained in all lockups. Written confirmation from client still outstanding.

column3: Design-forward premium beauty. Between clinical and lifestyle — earns its place through restraint, finish, and intention. Not trying to be Aesop, The Ordinary, or Grande.
column4: Clear white space in Australian lash serum market. No brand currently occupies design-led + genuine clinical credibility simultaneously.
column5: All visual decisions must be tested against this positioning. Anything reading as pharmacy or mass lifestyle is out.
column7: I feel like this language leans to conservative, boring... this brand still has an edge. it's cool!

column3: Aktiv Grotesk Extended. All-caps. Medium-bold weight. Geometric construction IS the character — not applied decoration. Extended, not condensed.
column4: Confirmed Mar 3. Fits brief: not neutral, not purely functional, chosen not defaulted to. Geometric construction is the point, not a compromise.
column5: Must be licensed before delivery. May still evolve slightly — identity not due yet. Tracking not yet decided.

column3: REJECTED. Mono / typewriter-adjacent secondary typeface will not be used. Rejected by Anh in the original strategy session.
column4: Anh confirmed she does not want mono or typewriter styles. This is not a Mar 3 decision — it goes back to the strategy session.
column5: Do not resurface this direction under any circumstances. It is a closed question.

column3: All-caps lockups throughout. Tracking not yet decided.
column4: ALT. as an abbreviation requires all-caps to hold structure and presence.
column5: Consistent across all touchpoints — tube, guidelines, collateral. Tracking decision deferred to identity refinement.

column3: Two-element identity system: hard geometric wordmark (primary) + ornamental secondary mark. These two elements should not look like they came from the same place — the tension is intentional.
column4: Contrast system inspired by atelier/silversmith tradition. Mark functions as a discovery element — found rather than announced.
column5: Ornamental mark must hold integrity at small scale and when debossed/hot-stamped. Needs internal logic specific to this brand.

column3: Ancient / Carved / Archaeological: Raw, primitive, worn-into-stone quality. Egyptian scarab seals, ancient intaglio gems. Incised not raised.
column4: One of three canonical directions being developed for client presentation. Surface feeling and register defines this direction.
column5: Legibility vs. abstraction is an independent axis — this direction can produce readable ALT. letterforms or a dissolved abstract form.

column3: Victorian / Script / Medieval: Fine copperplate script letterforms, flowing not rigid. Mauclot monogram sheet is the primary reference.
column4: Client preference for organic over structured engraving. Flowing, atelier-quality script register.
column5: Legibility vs. abstraction is an independent axis. Victorian structured heritage route is secondary to the organic preference.

column3: Architectural / Art Deco: Formal oval cartouche. Letters A, L, T positioned at compass points inside a double-ring oval. SRA SRA is the primary reference.
column4: SRA SRA mark = letters as separate positioned elements inside a structured architectural frame. NOT interlocked. Art deco lineage.
column5: Clean double-ring oval preferred over beaded variant. Legibility vs. abstraction deferred to client presentation.

column3: Hot-stamping or debossing onto primary tube component. Typography on packaging is marked, not printed.
column4: Letterform becomes a three-dimensional mark of quality — felt as much as seen. Industrial marker quality that signals authority.
column5: Need visual research into what hot-stamping and debossing look like on chrome surfaces. Must confirm compatibility with supplier.

column3: Reflective chrome is the confirmed primary packaging direction. This was never in question and is not an unresolved tension.
column4: Chrome/reflective finish communicates precision, structure, and industrial cool that subverts soft matte conventions of traditional beauty packaging.
column5: Not a blocker. Not a decision to be made. The bottle is chrome.

column3: Monochrome or near-neutral palette.  Probably black, white, silver. Earth tones unlikely.
column4: Anchored in material quality rather than colour expression. Colour should feel like it comes from the finish of the object.
column5: No colour exploration done yet. Vibrant accent colour not ruled out.
column7: I probably don't want earth tones..

column3: Acceptable for launch in current form. Long-term goal to transition to more sustainable packaging as brand scales. Avoid clear plastic where possible.
column4: Mecca has sustainability requirements — flagged as a future consideration, not a launch blocker.
column5: Slim metal tins identified as potentially stronger secondary packaging alternative. Gap between clean brand positioning and plastic is a known internal tension.
column7: Anh doesn't like the tins they're ruled out for now

column3: Precise, assured, uncluttered. States, doesn't sell. No exclamation marks. No wellness clichés (ritual, glow, nourishing). Specificity over vagueness. Authority without warmth.
column4: Voice must match visual identity — carries genuine authority without becoming cold or inaccessible.
column5: Four guiding principles: State don't sell. Specific over vague. Authority without warmth. Omission as a signal.
column7:
I think what I've tried to push a lot is ALT. doesn't conform to rules. I think all of this language is extremely limitting. it's very prescriptive on rules and don'ts. ALT can be cheeky, it can be flirty if it wants to. Sure it has a consistent "IFYKYK" and doesn't need to overexplain, but it also  could change and be more fun depending on the season, or based on product launches. I don't want a rigid set of rules... it feels tired.... 
*** that's a good thing to add in the brand stuff actually, seasonality - (huge in beauty) how ALT incorporates and adapts to the seasons.
